title: Log message template placeholders should be in the right order
description: The placeholders of a message template are defined by their name and their position. Log methods specify the values for the placeholder at runtime by passing them in a params array: logger.LogError("{First} placeholder and {Second} one.", first, second); This rule raises an issue if the position of an argument does not match the position of the corresponding placeholder: // 'first' and 'second' are swapped logger.LogError("{First} placeholder and {Second} one.", second, first); // ^^^^^^ ^^^^^ What is the potential impact? Logging providers use placeholder names to create key/value pairs in the log entry. The key corresponds to the placeholder and the value is the argument passed in the log call.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
// Noncompliant: Arguments are in wrong order
logger.LogError("{First} placeholder and {Second} one.", second, first); // Noncompliant
compliant:
// Compliant: Arguments match placeholder order
logger.LogError("{First} placeholder and {Second} one.", first, second); // Compliant

triggering_conditions: Placeholder names do not match argument names
Argument order does not match placeholder order
checks.reasoning: Logging providers create key/value pairs using placeholder names, so argument order must match placeholder order
